source_paper_title: Dynamic Portfolio Selection for Nonlinear Law-Dependent Preferences
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Liang_2025, title={Dynamic Portfolio Selection for Nonlinear Law-Dependent Preferences}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0345}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0345}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Liang, Zongxia and Xia, Jianming and Yuan, Fengyi}, year={2025}, month=june }
context:
Page 15, Section 6 (Remark 6.2(a))

It is worth noting that (6.4) is a two-dimensional QBSDE, whose two components \(\bar Y_1\) and \(\bar Y_2\) are coupled. Two-dimensional QBSDEs are technically much more challenging than the one-dimensional ones, and unless other assumptions are imposed, the well-posedness remains open; see the bibliographical notes in Subsection 7.4 of Zhang (2017).
title: Well-posedness theory for coupled multidimensional quadratic BSDEs in portfolio equilibria
background_latex:
Let 
\((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb F,\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a Brownian motion \(W\) on \([0,T]\), and consider backward stochastic differential equations (BSDEs) for unknown adapted processes \((Y,Z)\), where \(Y=(Y^1,\dots,Y^m)\) is \(\mathbb R^m\)-valued and \(Z=(Z^1,\dots,Z^m)\) with \(Z^i\in\mathbb R^d\). A (multi-dimensional) quadratic BSDE has the form
\[
Y_t = \xi + \int_t^T f(s,Y_s,Z_s)\,ds - \int_t^T Z_s\,dW_s,
\]
where the generator \(f\) has quadratic growth in \(Z\), e.g. \(|f(s,y,z)|\lesssim 1+|z|^2\). In many applications (including equilibrium characterizations in time-inconsistent portfolio selection with nonlinear law-dependent preferences), one obtains *coupled* systems where components \((Y^i,Z^i)\) interact through cross terms in \(Z\), yielding generators that are neither diagonally quadratic nor triangular.

A basic mathematical issue is *well-posedness*: existence and uniqueness of a solution \((Y,Z)\) in a natural solution class such as bounded \(Y\) and BMO-integrable \(Z\) (i.e., \(\int_0^\cdot Z_s\,dW_s\) is a BMO martingale). Current general results for multi-dimensional quadratic BSDEs typically require additional structural assumptions (e.g. diagonally quadratic or triangular generators) or smallness conditions on data.
problem_definition_latex: Develop general existence and uniqueness results for coupled multi-dimensional quadratic BSDE systems with genuinely coupled (cross-term) quadratic generators, beyond special structures such as diagonally quadratic or triangular forms, in a solution class such as \(Y\in L^\infty\) and \(Z\) BMO-integrable.
latex:
Let 
\((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb F,\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a Brownian motion \(W\) on \([0,T]\), and consider backward stochastic differential equations (BSDEs) for unknown adapted processes \((Y,Z)\), where \(Y=(Y^1,\dots,Y^m)\) is \(\mathbb R^m\)-valued and \(Z=(Z^1,\dots,Z^m)\) with \(Z^i\in\mathbb R^d\). A (multi-dimensional) quadratic BSDE has the form
\[
Y_t = \xi + \int_t^T f(s,Y_s,Z_s)\,ds - \int_t^T Z_s\,dW_s,
\]
where the generator \(f\) has quadratic growth in \(Z\), e.g. \(|f(s,y,z)|\lesssim 1+|z|^2\). In many applications (including equilibrium characterizations in time-inconsistent portfolio selection with nonlinear law-dependent preferences), one obtains *coupled* systems where components \((Y^i,Z^i)\) interact through cross terms in \(Z\), yielding generators that are neither diagonally quadratic nor triangular.

A basic mathematical issue is *well-posedness*: existence and uniqueness of a solution \((Y,Z)\) in a natural solution class such as bounded \(Y\) and BMO-integrable \(Z\) (i.e., \(\int_0^\cdot Z_s\,dW_s\) is a BMO martingale). Current general results for multi-dimensional quadratic BSDEs typically require additional structural assumptions (e.g. diagonally quadratic or triangular generators) or smallness conditions on data.

Develop general existence and uniqueness results for coupled multi-dimensional quadratic BSDE systems with genuinely coupled (cross-term) quadratic generators, beyond special structures such as diagonally quadratic or triangular forms, in a solution class such as \(Y\in L^\infty\) and \(Z\) BMO-integrable.
open_problem_quote: It is worth noting that (6.4) is a two-dimensional QBSDE, whose two components \(\bar Y_1\) and \(\bar Y_2\) are coupled. Two-dimensional QBSDEs are technically much more challenging than the one-dimensional ones, and unless other assumptions are imposed, the well-posedness remains open; see the bibliographical notes in Subsection 7.4 of Zhang (2017).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for a general theory guaranteeing existence/uniqueness, not a single falsifiable claim with a finite parameter search. Automated instance search might find pathological examples, but that would not resolve the well-posedness gap in either direction without substantial theoretical interpretation.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary coding loop could explore families of coupled quadratic generators and numerically solve the associated BSDEs to conjecture sharp smallness/structure conditions (e.g., bounds on BMO norms or monotonicity constants). However, the core contribution sought is a rigorous general theorem, and numerical fitness signals would only provide partial guidance.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Liang–Xia–Yuan, 2023) shows that equilibrium conditions for nonlinear law-dependent preferences can lead, in the weighted-utility case with random coefficients, to a genuinely coupled two-dimensional quadratic BSDE system with cross terms in \(Z\) (equations (6.3)–(6.4)). Their solvability result (Lemma 6.3) is obtained only under a smallness regime expressed via small BMO oscillation of \(\Theta=\int_0^T|\kappa_s|^2ds\), and it relies heavily on a special linear–quadratic structure plus equivalence of BMO norms under measure changes. This illustrates both the natural solution class \(Y\in L^\infty\), \(Z\in\mathrm{BMO}\) and the current limitation: outside special structure/smallness, well-posedness is not available.

Existing multidimensional quadratic BSDE theories provide well-posedness under structural restrictions: diagonally quadratic generators (Hu–Tang 2016; Fan–Hu–Tang 2023) and triangular generators (Jackson–Zitković 2022) yield global existence/uniqueness in \(S^\infty\times\mathrm{BMO}\), but do not cover the cross-coupled generators appearing in portfolio equilibria. For genuinely coupled systems, the main available tools are small-data/local results (Tevzadze 2008 in the scalar case; Frei 2014 via splitting; Kramkov–Pulido 2016 for a coupled system arising in price impact), typically requiring small terminal conditions, short horizons, or small BMO norms.

Recent application-driven work such as “Equilibrium Portfolio Selection under Utility-Variance Analysis of Log Returns in Incomplete Markets” further confirms the gap: it derives a coupled 2D quadratic BSDE for equilibria and proves well-posedness only when coupling disappears (\(\rho=0\)) or under bounded constraints, otherwise resorting to approximate equilibria. Alternative characterizations (e.g., deterministic integral equations in certain deterministic-coefficient settings) can bypass BSDEs but do not resolve the general coupled quadratic BSDE well-posedness question. Overall, the open problem remains to develop existence/uniqueness theory for fully coupled multidimensional quadratic BSDEs with cross-term quadratic dependence in \(Z\) in \(L^\infty\times\mathrm{BMO}\), without relying on diagonal/triangular structure or smallness assumptions.

sources.title: Dynamic portfolio selection for nonlinear law-dependent preferences
sources.theorem_or_result: Formulates equilibrium conditions for time-inconsistent portfolio choice under nonlinear law-dependent preferences and shows that, for weighted utility with random coefficients, the equilibrium is characterized by a genuinely coupled 2D quadratic BSDE system (equations (6.3)–(6.4)). Proves existence/uniqueness only under a smallness condition expressed as small BMO oscillation of \(\Theta=\int_0^T|\kappa_s|^2ds\) (Lemma 6.3), exploiting a special linear–quadratic structure and BMO norm equivalences under measure changes.
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem: it exhibits a coupled multidimensional quadratic BSDE with cross terms in \(Z\) arising from portfolio equilibria and explicitly notes that general well-posedness theory beyond diagonal/triangular structures is unavailable; its solvability result relies on special structure and a smallness regime, highlighting the gap the open problem asks to fill.

sources.title: Equilibrium Portfolio Selection under Utility-Variance Analysis of Log Returns in Incomplete Markets
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper derives a coupled two-dimensional quadratic BSDE system characterizing open-loop time-consistent equilibrium strategies for a utility–variance objective in an incomplete factor market (Theorem 3.1), and proves existence/verification of solutions only in special regimes: (i) when the Brownian motions are independent (correlation \(\rho=0\)), the system decouples and admits a unique bounded solution \((Y,Z)\in S^\infty\times L^2\) (Proposition 4.3) yielding an equilibrium strategy (Theorem 4.4); (ii) under a bounded trading constraint \(\pi_t\in A\), assuming a bounded BMO solution to the constrained coupled BSDE (4.11) exists (via Xing–Zitković (2018) AB/BF conditions), the projected feedback control (4.12) is an equilibrium (Theorem 4.11); (iii) for general \(\rho\neq 0\) without constraints, they cannot prove well-posedness, but construct an approximate equilibrium \(\pi^\rho\) with error \(O(\rho^2)\) (Theorem 5.6) based on solving a Markovian BSDE/PDE system (5.1) with bounded \(Y^\rho,Z^\rho\) under extra regularity (Proposition 5.5).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for general existence/uniqueness theory for genuinely coupled multidimensional quadratic BSDEs with cross-term quadratic dependence in \(Z\), in classes like bounded \(Y\) and BMO \(Z\), beyond diagonal/triangular structures or smallness assumptions. This paper provides an application-motivated example of such a coupled quadratic BSDE system (their equilibrium system (3.1)/(4.2)) and explicitly explains why standard multidimensional quadratic BSDE theories (fixed-point/triangular/AB-wAB approaches) do not apply in the fully coupled correlated case \(\rho\neq 0\) (Appendix C). It proves well-posedness only in special cases where coupling disappears (\(\rho=0\)) or is controlled by bounded constraints (projection onto a compact set), and otherwise replaces exact well-posedness by an \(O(\rho^2)\) approximate equilibrium. Thus it does not solve the general well-posedness problem, but contributes partial progress by isolating tractable regimes and clarifying structural obstructions in a concrete coupled quadratic system.

sources.title: An integral equation in portfolio selection with time-inconsistent preferences
sources.theorem_or_result:
They prove existence and uniqueness of a deterministic square-integrable solution to a nonlinear integral equation characterizing equilibrium strategies. Concretely, for bb\in L^2((0,T),\mathbb R^d), convex closed constraint sets \(U_t\subset\mathbb R^d\) with projection \(P_t\), and a function \(h\) that is I-bounded and locally Lipschitz, the equation
\[
a(t)=P_t\Big(h\big(t,\sqrt{\int_t^T |a(s)|^2ds},\int_t^T a(s)^\top\lambda(s)ds\big)\,\lambda(t)\Big),\quad t\in(0,T),
\]
admits a unique solution \(a\in L^2((0,T),\mathbb R^d)\) (Theorem 2.7), with local existence/uniqueness under local I-boundedness (Lemma 2.6, Theorem 2.5).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for well-posedness (existence/uniqueness) of genuinely coupled multidimensional quadratic BSDE systems in classes like bounded \(Y\) and BMO \(Z\). This paper does not study multidimensional quadratic BSDEs or BMO solutions. Instead it reduces certain time-inconsistent portfolio equilibrium conditions (in a deterministic-coefficient Brownian market) to a deterministic nonlinear integral equation for a function \(a(t)\), and proves well-posedness of that integral equation in \(L^2\). While such integral equations can sometimes be derived from (or related to) BSDE/HJB formulations, the results here do not provide a general BSDE well-posedness theory for coupled quadratic generators with cross terms.

sources.title: A system of quadratic BSDEs arising in a price impact model
sources.bibtex:
@article{KramkovPulido2016PriceImpactQuadraticBSDE,
  title   = {A system of quadratic {BSDE}s arising in a price impact model},
  author  = {Kramkov, Dmitry and Pulido, Sebastian},
  journal = {The Annals of Applied Probability},
  year    = {2016},
  volume  = {26},
  number  = {2},
  pages   = {794--817},
  doi     = {10.1214/15-AAP1122},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1214/15-AAP1122}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies a coupled system of multidimensional quadratic BSDEs motivated by equilibrium/price impact; proves solvability under smallness conditions using BMO techniques and fixed-point arguments in suitable norms.
sources.connection: Represents a canonical well-posedness result for coupled quadratic BSDE systems under smallness assumptions; it is one of the main methodological templates referenced in the source paper’s Section 6 for handling coupled quadratic terms, but does not cover general large-data genuinely coupled generators.

sources.title: Solvability of backward stochastic differential equations with quadratic growth
sources.bibtex:
@article{Tevzadze2008QuadraticGrowthBSDE,
  title   = {Solvability of backward stochastic differential equations with quadratic growth},
  author  = {Tevzadze, Revaz},
  journal = {Stochastic Processes and their Applications},
  year    = {2008},
  volume  = {118},
  number  = {3},
  pages   = {503--515},
  doi     = {10.1016/j.spa.2007.09.006},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1016/j.spa.2007.09.006}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides a foundational existence/uniqueness result for (primarily scalar) quadratic BSDEs and a small-terminal-condition approach that inspired later multidimensional small-data results via BMO and contraction mappings.
sources.connection: Although not a multidimensional coupled theory, it underpins the smallness/BMO-contraction methodology repeatedly used in attempts to treat coupled quadratic systems in equilibrium models.

sources.title: Splitting multidimensional BSDEs and finding local equilibria
sources.bibtex:
@article{Frei2014SplittingMultidimensionalBSDEs,
  title   = {Splitting multidimensional {BSDE}s and finding local equilibria},
  author  = {Frei, Carsten},
  journal = {Stochastic Processes and their Applications},
  year    = {2014},
  volume  = {124},
  number  = {8},
  pages   = {2654--2671},
  doi     = {10.1016/j.spa.2013.12.007},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1016/j.spa.2013.12.007}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops a splitting method for multidimensional BSDEs with quadratic growth, yielding local-in-time (or small-data) solvability for certain coupled systems and applications to local equilibria.
sources.connection: Provides partial progress toward coupled multidimensional quadratic BSDE well-posedness via decomposition/splitting, but still relies on locality/smallness and does not cover fully coupled global well-posedness in \(S^\infty\times\mathrm{BMO}\).

sources.title: Existence and uniqueness for non-markovian triangular quadratic bsdes
sources.bibtex:
@article{JacksonZitkovic2022TriangularQuadraticBSDE,
  title   = {Existence and uniqueness for non-markovian triangular quadratic {BSDE}s},
  author  = {Jackson, John and Zitkovi{\'c}, Gordan},
  journal = {SIAM Journal on Control and Optimization},
  year    = {2022},
  volume  = {60},
  number  = {3},
  pages   = {1642--1666},
  doi     = {10.1137/21M1410550},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1137/21M1410550}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves global existence and uniqueness for a broad class of multidimensional quadratic BSDEs with triangular structure in the generator, in \(S^\infty\times\mathrm{BMO}\), without smallness assumptions.
sources.connection: Represents the state of the art for multidimensional quadratic BSDE well-posedness under structural restrictions; the open problem asks to go beyond such triangular/diagonal structures to genuinely coupled cross-term generators.

sources.title: Multi-dimensional backward stochastic differential equations of diagonally quadratic generators
sources.bibtex:
@article{HuTang2016DiagonallyQuadraticBSDE,
  title   = {Multi-dimensional backward stochastic differential equations of diagonally quadratic generators},
  author  = {Hu, Ying and Tang, Shanjian},
  journal = {Stochastic Processes and their Applications},
  year    = {2016},
  volume  = {126},
  number  = {4},
  pages   = {1066--1086},
  doi     = {10.1016/j.spa.2015.12.004},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1016/j.spa.2015.12.004}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes existence/uniqueness for multidimensional quadratic BSDEs under a diagonally quadratic structure, controlling cross-dependence and using BMO estimates.
sources.connection: Shows that well-posedness is achievable under diagonal quadratic structure; however, the portfolio-equilibrium BSDEs in the source paper and related works violate diagonal structure due to cross terms, motivating the open problem.

sources.title: Multi-dimensional backward stochastic differential equations of diagonally quadratic generators: The general result
sources.theorem_or_result: Extends diagonally quadratic BSDE theory to more general settings, providing refined existence/uniqueness results under diagonal quadratic conditions.
sources.connection: Further advances the diagonal-structure theory but still does not address genuinely coupled cross-term quadratic generators arising in portfolio equilibria.
